candidates.studentStem: A:resurs80->54, miljö70->76, arter18->24. B:resurs80->83, miljö70->76, arter18->24. C:resurs80->54, miljö70->52, arter18->12. D:resurs80->83, miljö70->52, arter18->12. Vilken rad visar resursminskning och biologisk kostnad?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: C combines resource 80 to 54 with habitat 70 to 52 and species 18 to 12, so it is a resource gain with biological cost. A is resource gain with biological gain, B is resource cost with biological gain and D is resource cost with biological cost. (b) Distractor types: A is Type I positive-biology shortcut, B is Type II biology-only shortcut, D is Type III resource-direction mismatch. (c) Cognitive dimensions: two-direction comparison, habitat interpretation, species interpretation and distractor elimination. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests trade-off classification in sustainable production. (e) Knowledge link map: resource direction plus habitat and species direction. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: the direct C-row cue was removed; neutral option labels avoid keyword echo and the row must be inferred from data.

shortcut_check.reviewerNote: Pure text format is allowed here because the stem contains compact scenario or numeric data; the answer requires multiple inferences.

candidates.studentStem: A:v1 resurs 54 arter 24; v2 resurs 70 arter 19. B:v1 resurs 70 arter 24; v2 resurs 54 arter 19. C:v1 resurs 54 arter 19; v2 resurs 70 arter 24. D:v1 resurs 70 arter 19; v2 resurs 54 arter 24. Vilken rad ger v1 lägre resurs och fler arter?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: A gives product one resource 54 versus product two resource 70 and product one species 24 versus product two species 19. B keeps the species advantage but reverses resource direction, C keeps resource direction but reverses species direction, and D reverses both. Correct A is the only row with lower resource value and higher species value for product one. (b) Distractor types: B is Type I species-only shortcut, C is Type II resource-only shortcut, D is Type III double-reversal. (c) Cognitive dimensions: paired data comparison, resource-pressure reading, species-outcome reading and distractor elimination. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests biological product comparison from two measures. (e) Knowledge link map: resource direction plus species direction. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: neutral row labels and separated numeric values create explicit data load without keyword echo; the wording is under the pure-text word limit.

candidates.studentStem: A: åtg+6 ha, sårb 70->48, arter 18->22. B: åtg+6 ha, sårb saknas, arter 18->22. C: åtg startad, sårb 70->70, arter 18->18. D: åtg saknas, sårb 70->48, artlista 18. Vilken rad ger starkast stöd för effekt av åtgärden?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: A has an action of 6 ha, vulnerability 70 to 48 and species 18 to 22, so it gives the strongest evidence for an effect of the measure. B has the same action and species change but no vulnerability value, C has no outcome change, and D has lower vulnerability without an action and only a species list. (b) Distractor types: B is Type I missing-outcome shortcut, C is Type II action-label-without-effect shortcut, D is Type III outcome-without-action shortcut. (c) Cognitive dimensions: action-effect reasoning, vulnerability interpretation, species outcome interpretation, evidence completeness, bounded conclusion. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests evaluating whether action data and biological outcome data support a climate-adaptation effect. (e) Knowledge link map: action of 6 ha in A -> identify an actual measure -> needed to connect outcomes to an intervention; vulnerability 70 to 48 in A -> infer reduced consequence risk -> needed for the effect claim; species 18 to 22 in A -> include biological outcome evidence -> needed to avoid a risk-only answer; B/C/D missing or mismatched data -> reject incomplete proxies -> needed to eliminate distractors. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: unique goal wording was removed; all answer labels are neutral; the answer requires integrating action, vulnerability change, species change and distractor rejection. Can answer from text only? No. Can answer from one fact only? No.
